title: Coding Agent Security Checklist
concept: Verifying: execution sandboxed, resources capped, network restricted, linters active, outputs filtered.

exercises.prompt: What did we trace in the last lesson regarding how an agent resolves issues?

options.text: The steps agents take to solve problems automatically
The types of coding languages agents can use

exercises.explain: In the last lesson, we learned about the steps an agent takes to solve problems, which can help us understand how to code securely.

cards.title: Introduction to Security Checks
cards.body: When coding agents, safety is crucial. Security checks make sure that our agents operate safely and don't cause harm.

cards.title: What is a Security Checklist?
cards.body: A security checklist helps developers make sure that the coding environment for an agent is safe and controlled.

cards.title: Think of it Like a Fort
cards.body: Just as a fort has walls, guards, and rules to keep safe, an agent should have checks to ensure it runs securely and correctly.

cards.title: Security Checklist for Coding Agents
cards.body: Some key points include: 1. Execution sandboxed: separating code from the main system. 2. Resources capped: limiting what an agent can use.

cards.title: Myth: Security Checks are Optional
cards.body: Some may think that checking for security is not necessary. In reality, every agent needs these checks to prevent harmful errors.

cards.title: Visualize the Security Process
cards.body: Think of security checks as layers of protection for your code.

left.text: Execution is done in a sandbox to prevent outside interference.

right.text: Network access is controlled to prevent unauthorized connections.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is NOT a part of the security checklist for coding agents?
exercises.explain: User interface testing is not a part of the security checklist; it focuses more on how the code runs safely.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An execution sandbox allows code to interact freely with the main system.
exercises.explain: False! An execution sandbox restricts code interactions to keep the main system safe.

exercises.prompt: In coding agents, outputs should be __________ to prevent revealing sensitive information.
exercises.sentence: In coding agents, outputs should be filtered to prevent revealing sensitive information.
exercises.explain: Outputs should be 'filtered' to make sure sensitive information isn't exposed.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes a capped resource for coding agents?

options.text: An unlimited amount of data processing
Specs that control how much memory an agent can use
A feature that allows constant internet access

exercises.explain: Capping resources means setting limits, like controlling how much memory an agent can utilize.

exercises.prompt: Which combination of items is essential for a secure coding agent?

options.text: Execution sandboxed, network restricted, outputs filtered
Unlimited access, outputs filtered, no restrictions
Execution sandboxed, resource caps, open network
Outputs filtered, unlimited data usage, free execution

exercises.explain: The essential components for a secure coding agent are an execution sandbox, network restrictions, and filtered outputs to maintain security against risks.
